The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (H.R.1), signed into law on July 4, 2025, harmonized the domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y. This means that from June 16, 2025, onward, meeting domestic-content rules became a condition for obtaining the Section 45Y Production Tax Credit, making the requirements mandatory.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) introduced in 2025 does not include provisions making the existing domestic content bonus credit requirements mandatory for the Investment Tax Credits (ITC) as described under Section 48E. Instead, it shortens the deadline for the 45V hydrogen production credit, phases out solar and wind tax credits over two years and modifies domestic content percentages for the 48E investment credit. None of these provisions indicate a mandatory increase in domestic content requirements by 2025.

The House of Representatives passed H.R. 1 on May 22, 2025, and this bill imposes significant changes to the Inflation Reduction Act's clean energy provisions, including accelerated phase-outs and restrictions on certain tax credits, such as material assistance and foreign entity involvement.
